rationale: keys 9 and 10 sort by their 1-byte encoded form (0x09 < 0x0a), not numeric value -- happens to match numeric order here, the point is D3 sorts by bytes, not value. Mirrors rfc8949's P2, same underlying rule.

-24 encodes to 1 byte (0x37); 1000 encodes to 3 bytes (0x1903e8). D3 requires PURE bytewise order with no length pre-sort: 0x19 < 0x37, so the longer encoding of 1000 must sort before the shorter encoding of -24. A length-first (RFC 7049-style) comparator would wrongly sort -24 first.
